question_text: A microwave oven heats food by emitting microwaves with a frequency of 2.45 GHz. Calculate the wavelength of these microwaves. (Use the equation: wave speed = frequency × wavelength. The speed of all electromagnetic waves is 3 × 10⁸ m/s)
explanation: To find wavelength, rearrange the equation to: wavelength = wave speed ÷ frequency. First convert 2.45 GHz to 2.45 × 10⁹ Hz. Then: wavelength = (3 × 10⁸) ÷ (2.45 × 10⁹) = 0.122 m. This wavelength matches the size needed to excite water molecules in food.
